{"step_by_step_thinking": "Dysarthrosis refers to a disturbance in the production of speech, which can be due to damage to the muscles, nerves, or brain areas involved in speech production. In the case of a condylar fracture, it can lead to anatomical changes in the mandible or temporomandibular joint, which can affect the function of speech production. Therefore, dysarthrosis in this context would refer to a disturbance of both anatomy and function.", "answer_choice": "B"}
